\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{amsmath, amssymb, amsthm}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\usepackage{geometry}
\geometry{margin=1in}

\title{Erd\H{o}s Problem \#547}
\date{Last updated: 2025-09-11}
\author{Source: erdosproblems.com}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\noindent\textbf{Status:} OPEN \quad \textbf{No prize}

\noindent\textbf{Tags:} graph theory, ramsey theory

\medskip
\noindent\textbf{Problem Statement:}

If $T$ is a tree on $n$ vertices then\[R(T) \leq 2n-2.\]

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{Remarks:}

This follows directly from the conjecture of Erd\H{o}s and S\'{o}s in [548], and is therefore proved for all large $n$ assuming the announced proof of [548] by Ajtai, Koml\'{o}s, Simonovits, and Szemer\'{e}di, although this proof has not been published. Zhao \cite{Zh11} has proved $R(T)\leq 2n-2$ for all large $n$ via an alternative method.

If $T$ has a partition into two sets of vertices of sizes $t_1\geq t_2$ then Burr \cite{Bu74} showed\[R(T)\geq \max(t_1+2t_2,2t_1)-1,\]and conjectured this is sharp whenever $t_1\geq t_2\geq 2$. This strong conjecture was disproved by Grossman, Harary, and Klawe \cite{GHK79}.

Some results on Ramsey numbers of trees:
{UL}
{LI}When $T$ is a path on $n$ vertices Gerencs\'{e}r and Gy\'{a}rf\'{a}s \cite{GeGy67} proved $R(T)=\lfloor \frac{3}{2}n\rfloor-1$.{/LI}
{LI}When $T$ is the star $K_{1,n-1}$ Harary \cite{Ha72} proved $R(T)=2n-2$ if $n$ is even and $2n-3$ if $n$ is odd.{/LI}
{LI}When $T$ is the double star $S_{t_1,t_2}$, formed by joining the centres of two stars of sizes $t_1$ and $t_2$ by an edge, then when $t_1\geq 3t_2-2$ Grossman, Harary, and Klawe \cite{GHK79} proved $R(T)=2t_1$ (disproving Burr's conjecture).{/LI}
{LI}Norin, Sun, and Zhao \cite{NSZ16} proved that if $T$ is the double star $S_{2t,t}$ then $R(T)\geq (4.2-o(1))t$.{/LI}
{LI}Zhao \cite{Zh11} proved $R(T)\leq 2n-2$ for all large even $n$.{/LI}
{LI}Montgomery, Pavez-Sign\'{e}, and Yan \cite{MPY25} proved Burr's conjecture, that $R(T)=\max(2t_1,t_1+2t_2)-1$, if $T$ has maximum degree at most $cn$ for some constant $c>0$.{/LI}
{/UL}
This problem is #14 in Ramsey Theory in the graphs problem collection.

See also [549].

\bigskip
\noindent\textbf{References:}

[Bu74] Burr, Stefan A., Generalized {R}amsey theory for graphs---a survey. (1974), 52--75.
 
 [GHK79] Grossman, Jerrold W. and Harary, Frank and Klawe, Maria, Generalized {R}amsey theory for graphs. {X}. {D}ouble stars. Discrete Math. (1979), 247--254.
 
 [GeGy67] Gerencs\'{e}r, L. and Gy\'{a}rf\'{a}s, A., On {R}amsey-type problems. Ann. Univ. Sci. Budapest. E\"otv\"os Sect. Math. (1967), 167--170.
 
 [Ha72] Harary, Frank, Recent results on generalized {R}amsey theory for graphs. (1972), 125--138.
 
 [MPY25] R. Montgomery, M. Pavez-Sign\'{e}, and J. Yan, Ramsey numbers of trees. arXiv:2509.07934 (2025).
 
 [NSZ16] S. Norin and Y. R. Sun and Y. Zhao, Asymptotics of Ramsey numbers of double stars. arXiv:1605.03612 (2016).
 
 [Zh11] Zhao, Yi, Proof of the {$(n/2-n/2-n/2)$} conjecture for large {$n$}. Electron. J. Combin. (2011), Paper 27, 61.
